tsTroubleshooting.domains: (topic tsTroubleshooting) (topic hi-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic ut-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic sw-d) (topic ui-d) (topic oce-d) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c tsTroubleshooting oceTroubleshooting-c)
tsTroubleshooting.title: Usage of Engine Cycle ID (ECID)

abstract.p: 2 numbers are printed 51 cm (20") after the start of each print.
The position of the numbers (left and right hand side) is at the location of the AAC markers (Yellow or Black or White).
Together they form a 4 digit number which represents the Engine Cycle ID (ECID) : eg. 3146.

The ECID can be used for diagnostic and communication purposes. All internal stored data is linked to a specific ECID, therefore in case of an print quality complaints this number can speed up finding the right data of the mentioned print.
